title: dryopteris filix-mas var. fibrillosa
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas var. fibrillosa, also known as the Rough Buckler Fern, is a variety of the male fern plant that is native to Europe and Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing coughs, and aiding in digestion, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The Rough Buckler Fern can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal preparation methods.
